title: Calonema demissum
intro: Calonema demissum, also known as Indian gentian, is a plant species belonging to the Gentianaceae family, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Calonema demissum include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Calonema demissum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and powder extracts, often used as a tonic to boost energy and vitality.
